title: From Dreams to Reality: How to Set Bold Goals That Transform Your Life
guestBio: Wendee Close is an entrepreneur, mother of two, and founder of Goals2Life.com. She is a WBENC-certified business owner passionate about helping others transform their dreams into actionable steps and supporting women-owned businesses across the country.
excerpt: Discover how entrepreneur and mother Wendee Close turned her passion for goal-setting into a thriving business. Learn practical strategies for balancing leadership, family, and purpose while creating the life you truly want.
metaDescription: Learn how to set bold goals and live with purpose from entrepreneur Wendee Close on The Manage Her podcast. Actionable advice for women leaders.
keyTakeaways: Bold goals require both vision and actionable steps to transform dreams into reality.
Women-owned businesses thrive when supported by strong community networks and certification programs like WBENC.
Balancing motherhood and entrepreneurship is possible with clear priorities and purposeful living.
Success comes from aligning your business mission with your personal values and life purpose.
Goal-setting is a skill that can be learned and refined to create meaningful change in your life.
Supporting other women in business creates a ripple effect of empowerment and growth.
Living life on purpose means making intentional choices that reflect your deepest values.

pullQuotes.text: When you align your business mission with your personal values, success becomes inevitable.

timestamps.label: Final thoughts and where to connect

content:
What if the gap between your current reality and your biggest dreams isn't as wide as you think? What if it's simply a matter of having the right strategy, the right mindset, and the right support system to bridge that gap? In this powerful episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Wendee Close, a remarkable entrepreneur who has made it her mission to help others transform their aspirations into achievements.

Wendee Close isn't just another business coach talking about goal-setting from the sidelines. She's a mother of two who has walked the challenging path of building a purpose-driven business while maintaining her commitment to family. As the founder of Goals2Life.com and a WBENC-certified business owner, Wendee brings both personal experience and professional expertise to the conversation about what it really takes to live life on your own terms.

The Journey from Vision to Reality

Every successful entrepreneur has a moment when they realize their calling extends beyond personal achievement to serving others. For Wendee, that moment came when she recognized the profound gap between what people dream of accomplishing and what they actually achieve. Too many brilliant, capable women were stuck in the planning phase, never making the leap from intention to action.

Through Goals2Life.com, Wendee has created a framework that transforms the often overwhelming process of goal-setting into manageable, actionable steps. This isn't about creating another vision board or writing down wishes. This is about developing the systematic approach that turns your boldest aspirations into your lived reality.

The beauty of Wendee's approach lies in its practicality. She understands that for busy women juggling multiple responsibilities, goal-setting strategies need to be both powerful and sustainable. You don't have time for complicated systems that require hours of daily maintenance. You need something that works with your life, not against it.

The Power of Community and Certification

One of the most compelling aspects of Wendee's journey is her commitment to supporting women-owned businesses through her WBENC certification. The Women's Business Enterprise National Council certification isn't just a badge of honor; it's a gateway to opportunities and a symbol of the collective strength that emerges when women support other women in business.

This certification represents something deeper than individual achievement. It's about recognizing that when women succeed in business, they create ripple effects that benefit entire communities. They become employers, mentors, and examples for the next generation of female leaders. They prove that it's possible to build something meaningful while staying true to your values.

Wendee's commitment to this certification reflects her understanding that true success isn't just personal – it's communal. When you support women-owned businesses, you're investing in a future where female entrepreneurship is not the exception but the norm.

Balancing Leadership and Motherhood

Perhaps one of the most relatable aspects of Wendee's story is her honest approach to balancing motherhood with business leadership. As a mother of two, she knows firsthand the unique challenges that women face when trying to build something significant while raising children. This isn't about having it all or doing it all perfectly. It's about making intentional choices that honor both your family commitments and your professional aspirations.

The conversation reveals that successful work-life integration isn't about perfect balance – it's about purposeful prioritization. Some seasons require more focus on family, while others allow for intensive business growth. The key is maintaining clarity about your values and making decisions that align with your long-term vision for both your family and your career.

Wendee's example shows that motherhood doesn't have to be a barrier to entrepreneurial success. Instead, it can be a source of motivation, creativity, and resilience. The skills you develop as a mother – multitasking, problem-solving, patience, and unconditional commitment – are exactly the skills that serve you as a business leader.

Living Life on Purpose

At its core, this episode is about more than business strategies or goal-setting techniques. It's about the fundamental choice to live life on purpose rather than by default. Wendee's journey illustrates what becomes possible when you align your professional work with your personal mission.

Living life on purpose means making conscious decisions about how you spend your time, energy, and resources. It means saying no to opportunities that don't serve your larger vision and saying yes to the work that lights you up, even when it feels scary or uncertain. It means believing that your dreams are not just wishful thinking but blueprints for the life you're meant to create.

The transformation that Wendee facilitates through her work isn't just about achieving specific goals. It's about becoming the kind of person who sets bold goals and follows through on them. It's about developing the confidence, skills, and mindset that make seemingly impossible dreams feel not only achievable but inevitable.

This episode reminds us that you don't have to choose between being a devoted mother and a successful entrepreneur, between serving others and achieving personal fulfillment, between dreaming big and being practical. With the right approach, the right support, and the right commitment to living on purpose, you can create a life that honors all aspects of who you are and who you're becoming.
